name: NRT2.1p::Betanin::tNOS — Nitrate Reporter Construct
description: A visual reporter construct for detecting nitrate levels in soil using a color-change readout in Arabidopsis thaliana.

promoter.name: AtNRT2.1 promoter
promoter.rationale: NRT2.1 is a high-affinity nitrate transporter induced under low nitrate conditions. Its promoter provides nitrate-responsive transcriptional control with well-characterized dose-response behavior.

reporter.name: Betanin biosynthesis cassette (CYP76AD1, DODA1, cDOPA5GT)
reporter.rationale: Betanin produces visible red-violet pigment detectable by eye without laboratory equipment. Ideal for field-deployable biosensors. Three-gene cassette enables complete betalain pathway.

terminator.name: NOS terminator
terminator.rationale: Standard reliable terminator with extensive track record in plant transformation vectors.

regulatory_elements.name: TMV Omega leader
regulatory_elements.rationale: Translational enhancer to boost betanin cassette expression for stronger visible signal.

transduction: CIPK23 phosphorylation modulates NRT1.1 affinity switching
NLP7 transcription factor activated by nitrate signaling
NLP7 binds nitrate-responsive elements (NREs) in NRT2.1 promoter
NRT2.1 promoter drives betanin cassette expression

dose_response.measurement: RGB color quantification from leaf images

controls.negative: NRT2.1p::GUS::tNOS (non-color reporter for promoter activity verification)

specificity_tests: Phosphate starvation — should NOT trigger reporter
Potassium deficiency — should NOT trigger reporter

literature_support.note: Original characterization of NRT2.1 nitrate-inducible expression
NRT2.1 promoter regulation by nitrogen status
Betanin as a visible reporter in plants
